input.old_string:
## Request mocking for tests

Playwright tests use
[`request-mocking-protocol`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/request-mocking-protocol)
(RMP) to stub server-side `fetch` calls without a live backend.

- The Playwright fixture in `tests/test-fixture.ts` forwards the
  `x-mock-request` header (`RMP_MOCK_HEADER` in `src/mocks/rmp-constants.ts`)
  from browser to server and **blocks all cross-origin browser requests** —
  external APIs must go through server functions for tests to pass.
- Server-side mocking is gated by `VITE_ENABLE_RMP=true`. When enabled,
  `src/router.tsx` installs `setupRequestMocking` (SSR only) and
  `src/server/org-api.server.ts` forwards `x-mock-request` to the upstream
  Org API fetch.
input.new_string:
## Request mocking for tests

Playwright tests use
[`request-mocking-protocol`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/request-mocking-protocol)
(RMP) to stub server-side `fetch` calls without a live backend.

- The Playwright fixture in `tests/test-fixture.ts` forwards the
  `x-mock-request` header (`RMP_MOCK_HEADER` in `src/mocks/rmp-constants.ts`)
  from browser to server and **blocks all cross-origin browser requests** —
  external APIs must go through server functions for tests to pass.
- Server-side mocking is gated by `VITE_ENABLE_RMP=true`. When enabled,
  `src/router.tsx` installs `setupRequestMocking` (SSR only) and
  `src/server/org-api.server.ts` forwards `x-mock-request` to the upstream
  Org API fetch.
- `tests/test-fixture.ts` overrides `page.goto` to `await` a
  `window.__HYDRATED__` flag set by the `useEffect` in `src/routes/__root.tsx`.
  Tests must use this fixture's `page` (not a raw Playwright page) so clicks
  and fills never race React hydration — without this, submit buttons do
  native GET submissions and dropdown/dialog triggers no-op.
- `root-error.spec.ts` intentionally mocks `/users/me` as 500, which causes
  the server to log `OrgApiError: Internal Server Error` via
  `logServerErrorToRollbar` during the test run. This log line is expected
  test noise, not a broken mock.

## Vendored dependency alignment

`vite.config.ts` sets `resolve.dedupe = ["lucide-react", "react",
"react-dom"]`. `packages/ui` currently pins `lucide-react@^0.486` while
`apps/org-next` is on `^0.577`, so without dedupe the SSR and client
module graphs resolve different copies. lucide-react started emitting a
default `aria-hidden="true"` attribute around 0.5x, which surfaces as a
React hydration attribute mismatch on every SSR-ed icon and breaks event
handler attachment on the affected subtree (sidebar triggers, submit
buttons, dialog/dropdown triggers). Keep this dedupe entry — or bump
`packages/ui`'s lucide-react to match — until the workspace is on a single
version.

content.text: Let me verify the full test suite one last time, then summarize:

input.command: cd /Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next && rm -rf test-results/ && timeout 120 pnpm exec playwright test --retries=0 --reporter=line 2>&1 | tail -5

content.text:
All 11 e2e tests pass (~6.4s). Your intuition was right — the issues all trace back to one compound root cause in recent refactor commits.

## Root cause

Two issues layered together, both introduced by `020148f refactor(org-next): replace app header with persistent sidebar layout and stabilize e2e setup`:

1. **Hydration mismatch from duplicate `lucide-react`** — `packages/ui` pins `lucide-react@^0.486` but `apps/org-next` uses `^0.577`. The newer version auto-injects `aria-hidden="true"` on SSR-rendered icons; the older one does not. SSR and client bundles were resolving different copies, producing a mismatch on the SidebarTrigger's `<ChevronLeft>`. When React 19 hits a "won't be patched up" mismatch, it bails out of attaching event handlers on that subtree — so submit buttons fell through to native form submission (the `?name=Acme+co` URLs), dropdown triggers no-op'd, and dialogs never opened.

2. **Tests never wait for hydration** — That same commit added a `window.__HYDRATED__` flag in `src/routes/__root.tsx` and removed `waitUntil: "networkidle"` from every test, but forgot to wire the flag into the fixture. So `page.goto` returned before hydration finished and every subsequent `click`/`fill` raced React.

The `OrgApiError: Internal Server Error` logs you saw are NOT broken mocks — they're from `tests/root-error.spec.ts`, which deliberately mocks `/users/me` as 500 to exercise the root error boundary. The RMP mock was working correctly; the server just logs any 500 response through `logServerErrorToRollbar`.

## Fixes

- `apps/org-next/vite.config.ts` — added `resolve.dedupe: ["lucide-react", "react", "react-dom"]` so SSR and client resolve the same copies.
- `apps/org-next/tests/test-fixture.ts` — wrapped `page.goto` to `await page.waitForFunction(() => window.__HYDRATED__ === true)` after each navigation, so every test automatically waits for hydration without needing per-test `networkidle` flags.
- `apps/org-next/docs/README.md` — documented both assumptions (hydration-wait fixture contract, lucide-react dedupe requirement, and the expected `root-error.spec.ts` log noise) per the repo's `docs/` policy.

Stability check: 3 consecutive full runs at `workers=5`, 11/11 passing each run in ~6.3–6.4s.

Exclude one-off/transient details and secrets. If no meaningful updates exist, respond exactly: No high-signal memory updates.
